project.name: CodeSense
project.description: An AI Agent that analyzes any programming file, breaks it into logical segments, and teaches the user how every part works through an interactive chat tutor with a customizable personality.
project.purpose: To fight AI-induced passivity by making people actually understand the code they write or use, rather than blindly copying from AI tools.

ai.purpose: Code explanation, segment parsing for non-Python files, interactive chat

backend: app.py              — Flask server, all API routes
frontend: static/index.html   — Main HTML file, all pages
static/script.js    — All interactivity and API calls

page_1_hero.name: Hero

content: Website name — CodeSense in large gold typography
Subheading — Learn to build with AI in white
Short paragraph — describing what CodeSense does
CTA button — Get Started scrolls to upload section
Animated visual — floating code particles or a glowing orb
Animation style — CSS keyframes with gold and cyan colors
Could show rotating code snippets in different languages

animations: Hero title fades in on load

page_2_upload.name: Upload
page_2_upload.trigger: Scroll down from hero or click Get Started

content: Square file upload box with dashed gold border
Upload icon in the center of the box
Drag and drop supported
Click to browse files also supported
Text below box — Upload any programming file

states.file_loaded: Shows filename and file size, green checkmark

content: Rectangle text input for personality
Placeholder — e.g. Friendly big brother, Strict professor...
Text below input — Set any personality for your AI tutor
Analyze Code button below in gold

sections.content: Why CodeSense was built — the problem of AI-induced passivity and people not understanding their own code
Three simple steps — Upload, Choose Personality, Learn. Visual step cards with gold icons.

content: Supports 14+ programming languages
Customizable AI tutor personality
Interactive segment-by-segment explanation
Built-in chat for follow-up questions
Downloadable PDF and Word report
GitHub link — TBD
Email — TBD
Built with Groq + LLaMA 3.3

page_4_working.name: Working
page_4_working.trigger: Only appears after clicking Analyze Code button
page_4_working.description: This page replaces the scroll layout entirely. User is taken here after analysis is complete.

left_side.name: Code Panel
content: Full code of the uploaded file
Syntax highlighted using Highlight.js
Line numbers shown
Scrollable if code is long
Language tag shown at top right of panel

right_side.name: Segments Panel
content: List of all segments found in the code
Each segment shown as a card
Each card has the segment name and type
Each card has an info button on the right

chatbox.trigger: Clicking the ℹ️ info button on any segment
behavior: Opens with a smooth fade-in animation
First message is auto-generated explanation of the segment
AI responds in the chosen personality
Full conversation history maintained per segment
Clicking outside the chatbox closes it smoothly
Reopening same segment restores previous conversation

content: CodeSense logo on the left
Filename shown in the center
Download Report button on the right (PDF or Word)

POST /analyze.description: Receives code file and personality, returns parsed segments

output.segments: List of segment objects with type, name, code, children, lines

POST /explain.description: Receives a segment and returns an initial explanation

output.explanation: AI generated explanation of the segment

POST /chat.description: Handles follow-up chat messages within a segment chatbox

output.reply: AI response to the user's question

POST /download.description: Generates and returns a PDF or Word report of all explanations

code_parsing.python: Uses Python ast module to extract top-level and nested functions and classes
code_parsing.other_languages: Uses Groq AI to identify logical segments and return structured JSON

explanation_levels.whole_segment: 2-3 sentence summary of what the whole segment does
explanation_levels.interactive_chat: Full conversation with the AI about the segment with follow-up questions

chat_memory.scope: Per segment — each segment has its own conversation history
chat_memory.storage: JavaScript object in the browser, keyed by segment name
chat_memory.persistence: Lives for the duration of the session only

mvp: File upload — drag and drop or click to browse
Code segmentation — Python ast + AI for other languages
Full code view with syntax highlighting
Download report as PDF or Word
future_versions: Multiple file upload and project-wide analysis
GitHub repository URL input — analyze entire repos
Segment relationship diagram — visual map of how segments connect
Quiz mode — AI tests your understanding after explanation
Voice tutor — AI explains code out loud

build_order.description: Set up Flask server with all four API routes
Code parser — Python ast + AI parser for other languages
All Groq API functions — explain, chat, parse non-Python
All four pages — Hero, Upload, About, Working
Full black gold cyan theme, animations, responsive layout
All interactivity — file upload, API calls, chatbox, segment rendering
Upload a real file, analyze, chat, download report
Initialize git repo, add files, push to GitHub

key_principles: Test each API route before building the UI
Every feature should work before moving to the next
Mobile responsiveness is optional for MVP
